_doc: Per-image Gemini prompts for The 500+ Whop Stores Index. {palette} resolves at runtime. Cover concept: a stylized 5x5 mosaic of small 'store card' thumbnails (25 cells), each cell a small rounded rectangle with a tiny abstract line-art icon, alternating off-white outline and faint cyan-tinted fill, telegraphing a marketplace of many shops.

cover.prompt:
Editorial book cover for a downloadable lead magnet PDF. Aspect: portrait, 3:4, US letter book cover proportions.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed under TEXT TO RENDER below. No other text. No font names. No instructions. No labels beyond what is listed.
2. Never render the words 'Inter', 'Inter Regular', 'Inter Display Bold', 'JetBrains Mono', 'sans serif', or any typography or font instruction inside the image.
3. Spell every rendered word exactly as written, character for character.
4. The mosaic of small store-card cells must NOT contain any text, brand names, real logos, or labels of any kind. Cells contain ONLY tiny abstract line-art icons. No dollar signs as text. No store names. No URL fragments.
5. The icons inside the cells are abstract shape-icons only, not realistic illustrations.

TEXT TO RENDER (exactly these pieces, nothing more):
A. Top-left small pill, uppercase, two words: FREE PLAYBOOK
B. Upper-third headline across two lines: line 1 reads 'The 500+', line 2 reads 'Whop Stores Index'.
C. Subtitle inside a horizontal off-white translucent panel directly below the headline, one short line: 512 stores. 20 niches. The 6 monetization shapes. The 60-day launch plan.
D. Bottom-left small wordmark: AI BrandFactory

CENTRAL VISUAL (this is the hero of the cover, fills the lower 60 percent of the cover):
A stylized 5-column by 5-row mosaic grid of 25 small 'store card' thumbnails, evenly spaced with small consistent gutters between cells. The grid is centered horizontally, dominates the lower 60 percent of the cover.

Each of the 25 cells is a small rounded rectangle with these properties:
- Rounded corners, all cells the same size and shape.
- Cells alternate between two visual treatments in a checkerboard-like pattern (not strictly checkerboard, more loosely alternating to feel hand-curated rather than algorithmic):
  * TYPE A cells: thin off-white #FAF9FF outline at about 70 percent opacity, dark indigo interior fill, no glow.
  * TYPE B cells: faint bright cyan #06B6D4 tinted interior fill at about 18 to 25 percent saturation, off-white outline at about 60 percent opacity.
- Inside each cell, centered, a tiny abstract line-art icon rendered in thin off-white #FAF9FF or thin bright cyan #06B6D4 stroke (1 to 1.5 pixel weight). The icons vary across cells. Examples of acceptable abstract icon shapes (use a varied mix across the 25 cells, do not repeat any one icon more than twice):
  * a small abstract chart/candle shape
  * a small dumbbell-like horizontal-bar-with-circles
  * a small question-mark glyph
  * a small coin/circle with inner ring
  * a small abstract brain (a half-circle with internal squiggle)
  * a small paint-brush stroke
  * a small game-controller silhouette (rectangular with two circles)
  * a small microphone (rectangle on a stand)
  * a small camera shape (rectangle with circle)
  * a small calendar grid (small 3x3 dots)
  * a small lightning bolt
  * a small house outline
  * a small abstract arrow (up-right diagonal)
  * a small chat-bubble outline
  * a small atom/orbit shape (circle with 2 elliptical rings)
  * a small target (concentric 3 circles)
  * a small bar-chart (3 rising vertical bars)
  * a small wave/heartbeat line
  * a small puzzle piece outline
  * a small key shape
  * a small leaf outline
  * a small clock face (circle with two hands)
  * a small infinity loop
  * a small star (5-point outline)
  * a small shield outline
Use a varied mix from this list (and similar abstract shapes) across the 25 cells. Each icon is small enough to read as a 'category symbol' inside its cell, never overflowing the cell, never with text.
- 4 to 6 of the cells (scattered across the grid, not clustered) have a slightly brighter cyan glow ring around the cell outline, telegraphing them as 'featured' stores in the index. The glow is subtle, not overwhelming.

Overall the 5x5 mosaic reads as 'a marketplace of many shops, each different, all part of one index.'

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Cover headline lines 1 and 2: large bold sans-serif display face, off-white #FAF9FF, tight letter spacing. Line 1 ('The 500+') at slightly larger weight as the visual anchor of the headline.
- Subtitle panel: clean medium-weight sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside the off-white panel.
- Top-left pill: condensed mono-spaced face, bright cyan #06B6D4.
- Wordmark: clean light-weight sans-serif, off-white.

Design language:
- Premium editorial cover with a stylized marketplace-mosaic composition as the topic-specific centerpiece.
- Background: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient at 135 degrees, subtle bright cyan bloom upper right.
- Layout: pill + headline + subtitle panel in upper third, the 5x5 mosaic dominates the lower 60 percent, wordmark anchored bottom-left.
- Subtle paper grain texture across the whole frame.
- The mosaic is the topic-specific central composition that telegraphs 'a curated index of many stores,' echoing the system-illustration approach of P09 Multi-Agent Playbook (a hexagon-and-nodes diagram) with a 5x5 store-card mosaic replacing the hexagon network.

Color palette: {palette}.

What to avoid:
- No diagonal cyan accent line crossing the frame (was P32, P33, P35 default, retired).
- No 'WHAT IS INSIDE' grid of text pills.
- No funnel composition (P38).
- No concentric rings composition (P37).
- No horizontal stacked-bars composition (P36).
- No vintage ad mockups or fanned card stack (P39).
- No location-pin network (P40).
- No browser-window chrome (P42).
- No soundwave / waveform composition.
- No 5-layer architecture stack (P50).
- No silhouette of a person.
- No 12-cell 4x3 grid (P51 used that). This is a 5x5 grid of 25 cells, not 4x3 of 12.
- No 3-step sequence composition.
- No post-card cascade (P34).
- No flow / arrow / pipeline composition.
- No text inside the mosaic cells. No store names. No real Whop store URLs visible. No dollar signs as readable text.
- No realistic clip art or photographic icons inside the cells. The icons are abstract line-art symbols only.
- No real Whop logo, no real Discord/Telegram/Stripe/Skool/Circle logos.
- No human figures, no faces, no avatars, no illustrations of people.
- No emoji icons.
- No realistic illustrations of laptops, phones, screens, or apps.

Output: a single high-quality cover image, print-ready, 4K.

hero.prompt:
Wide hero banner for a squeeze page giving away 'The 500+ Whop Stores Index'. Aspect: 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
- NO rendered text anywhere in the image.
- No font names or typography labels.
- No store names, no Whop URLs, no labels of any kind in the mosaic cells.
- The icons inside any cell are abstract line-art shapes only, never realistic icons or text.

Composition: a 3D-rendered isometric mockup of a US-letter PDF cover floating on the right third, soft drop shadow on a textured editorial background. The PDF cover shows the same 5x5 mosaic composition from the cover design (small store-card cells with abstract line-art icons inside).

On the left two-thirds: an abstract version of the store-card mosaic motif rendered at low opacity. A loose 4x4 or 5x4 arrangement of small rounded-rectangle cells in off-white #FAF9FF outlines and faint bright cyan #06B6D4 tinted fills, distributed across the area with comfortable gutters. Inside about half of these visible cells, tiny abstract line-art icons in thin off-white or thin bright cyan stroke (charts, dumbbells, question marks, coins, brushes, controllers, etc, varied). 2 to 4 of the cells have a subtle bright cyan glow ring around the outline, the visual focal points of the abstract motif.

Background:
- Top half: deep indigo #1A0B4B fading to electric purple #7C3AED.
- Bottom half: off-white #FAF9FF with faint mesh gradient bloom.
- PDF mockup on the dividing line.
- Subtle paper grain.

Style:
- Premium editorial, restrained.
- No people, no faces, no brand logos, no real Whop logo, no Discord/Telegram/Skool/Circle logos, no padlock or shield icons, no realistic clip-art icons inside cells.
- The store-card mosaic is the recurring motif, rendered subtly so the PDF mockup remains the focal point.
- No diagonal cyan accent line. No funnel. No concentric rings. No vintage ad mockup card stack. No location-pin network. No browser window. No 12-cell 4x3 grid (this composition uses 5x5 or near-5x5 mosaic, not 4x3).

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single 4K hero image.

og.prompt:
Open Graph social share card image, 1.91:1 widescreen.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. Contains ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. No font names or typography instructions.
3. Spell every word exactly as written.
4. NO store names, no Whop URLs, no real brand names, no labels of any kind in the mosaic area on the right.
5. Icons inside the mosaic cells (if visible) are abstract line-art shapes only, not realistic icons.

TEXT TO RENDER:
A. Top-left small pill, uppercase: AI BRANDFACTORY
B. Centered massive headline two lines: line 1 reads 'The 500+', line 2 reads 'Whop Stores Index'. Line 2 should be smaller than line 1.
C. Below headline, off-white rounded subtitle pill, one line: 512 stores cataloged across 20 niches with the 6 monetization shapes and the 60-day launch plan
D. Below subtitle pill, horizontal text strip with middle-dot separators: Whop Economy · 6 Shapes · 20-Niche Map · Pattern Framework · Pricing Zones · Top 50 · 60-Day Plan
E. Bottom-right small label, faded: files.aibrandfactory.com

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top-left pill: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4.
- Headline line 1: massive bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF.
- Headline line 2: smaller medium-weight sans-serif, off-white at 80% opacity.
- Subtitle: clean medium-weight sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside pill.
- Stages strip: clean medium-weight sans-serif, bright cyan #06B6D4, smaller size.
- Bottom-right label: clean sans-serif, off-white 70%.

Background: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient at 135 degrees. On the right third, an abstract version of the 5x5 store-card mosaic motif: a loose grid of small rounded-rectangle cells in off-white #FAF9FF outlines and faint bright cyan tinted fills. Inside about half of the visible cells, tiny abstract line-art icons in thin off-white or bright cyan stroke (varied abstract shapes, no realistic icons, no text). 2 to 3 cells have soft bright cyan glow rings around the outlines as the visual focal points.

What to avoid:
- No people, no faces, no brand logos, no real Whop logo, no Discord/Telegram logos.
- No additional text beyond the pieces listed.
- No store names, no Whop URLs, no real brand names in the mosaic area.
- No realistic clip art icons inside the cells.
- No diagonal cyan accent line motif. No funnel composition (P38). No vintage ad mockup card stack (P39). No concentric rings (P37). No location-pin network (P40). No browser window (P42). No 12-cell 4x3 grid (P51).

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: sharp, ready as og:image.

notion-cover.prompt:
Wide editorial banner for a Notion page header. Aspect 21:9.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: NO title, NO headline, NO wordmark, NO logo, NO labels in the corners. NO legible English words anywhere in the image. NO store names, no Whop URLs, no real brand names in the mosaic. Icons inside the mosaic cells are abstract line-art shapes only, never realistic icons or text.

Composition:
- Background: deep indigo #1A0B4B on the left fading to electric purple #7C3AED on the right, subtle bright cyan #06B6D4 bloom upper right.
- Across the full frame edge-to-edge, distributed across the banner: a horizontally-stretched mosaic of small rounded-rectangle 'store card' cells (roughly 4 to 5 rows by 12 to 15 columns to fit the wide aspect), in off-white #FAF9FF outlines and faint bright cyan #06B6D4 tinted interior fills (alternating loosely between the two treatments).
- Inside about half of the visible cells, tiny abstract line-art icons in thin off-white or thin bright cyan stroke (1 to 1.5 pixel weight). Varied abstract shapes only (charts, dumbbells, question marks, coins, brushes, controllers, microphones, calendars, lightning, houses, arrows, chat bubbles, orbits, targets, bar charts, waves, puzzle pieces, keys, leaves, clocks, stars, shields, etc). No icon repeats more than 3 times across the banner. Each icon stays well within its cell, never overflowing.
- 5 to 8 cells scattered across the banner have soft bright cyan glow rings around their outlines as visual focal points.
- Subtle paper grain across the entire banner.

What to avoid:
- No corner text. No wordmark. No logos.
- No real Whop logo, no Discord/Telegram/Skool/Circle logos. No real brand logos of any kind.
- No legible English text or labels anywhere. No store names. No Whop URLs.
- No realistic clip-art icons inside cells. The icons are abstract line-art symbols only.
- No emoji.
- No diagonal accent line crossing the frame.
- No padlock or shield icons as a featured/realistic element.
- No funnel composition (P38). No concentric rings composition (P37). No vintage ad mockup card stack (P39). No location-pin network (P40). No browser window (P42).
- No 12-cell 4x3 grid like P51. This banner uses a horizontally-stretched mosaic of 4-5 rows x 12-15 columns to suit the 21:9 aspect.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 4K cinematic banner.
